findings.title: Hardcoded API key in source
findings.evidence: The raw diff adds `apiKey: "sk-abcdefghijklmnop"` as a literal string property in the exported `provider` object. This key is committed directly in source and would be exposed in version history, CI logs, and any downstream consumer. The Diff Drift report independently flags this as high severity with matching evidence.

notes: The `sk-` prefix pattern is consistent with common API key conventions (e.g. OpenAI, Stripe), suggesting this is a real credential format rather than a placeholder — though the value itself may be synthetic in this eval context.
Only one file changed in this diff; the hardcoded secret is the sole risk.
Remediation: replace the literal with `process.env.PROVIDER_API_KEY` or equivalent secret-store injection, and rotate the exposed key if it was ever deployed.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
